Data Structures and Algorithms: Mastery of core data structures—arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, hash tables, trees, and graphs—is fundamental to writing efficient software. Algorithm design paradigms including divide-and-conquer, dynamic programming, and greedy approaches apply across a wide range of computational problems. Big-O notation provides a language for reasoning about time and space complexity independent of hardware. Regular practice on problems from platforms like LeetCode or HackerRank reinforces pattern recognition for technical interviews.
